title: Hasbro (HAS) Down 11% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Rebound?
content:
A month has gone by since the last earnings report for Hasbro (HAS). Shares have lost about 11% in that time frame, underperforming the S&P 500.

Will the recent negative trend continue leading up to its next earnings release, or is Hasbro due for a breakout? Well, first let's take a quick look at the most recent earnings report in order to get a better handle on the recent catalysts for Hasbro, Inc. before we dive into how investors and analysts have reacted as of late.

Hasbro Q4 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates

Hasbro reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results, with earnings and revenues beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top and bottom lines increased year over year. 

Hasbro’s management highlighted a strong 2025, with it returning to growth on the back of disciplined execution and the “Playing to Win” strategy. Leadership emphasized broader fan engagement, new partnerships and meaningful progress toward becoming a more digital and IP-focused business, setting a confident tone for 2026.

From a financial perspective, cost-saving and transformation efforts supported improved performance, with Wizards of the Coast emerging as the key driver, led by record Magic revenues. Management also reaffirmed its commitment to shareholder returns, pointing to a planned $1 billion share repurchase while continuing to invest in the business.

Hasbro’s Q4 Earnings & Revenues

In fourth-quarter fiscal 2025, Hasbro reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.51, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 99 cents. In the year-ago quarter, it reported an adjusted EPS of 46 cents.

Net revenues of $1,445.9 million beat the consensus mark of $1,288 million. Moreover, the top line rose 31.3% from $1,101.6 million reported in the prior-year period.

Hasbro’s Segmental Revenues

Hasbro has three reportable operating segments, Consumer Products, Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming, and Entertainment.

In the fiscal fourth quarter, net revenues from the Consumer Products segment rose 7.2% year over year to $800 million. Our model predicted the segment’s revenues to be $716.2 million. Adjusted operating margin declined 10.1% year over year.

The Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming segment’s revenues totaled $630.4 million, up 86% from $339 million reported in the year-ago quarter. Our model predicted the segment’s revenues to be $520.2 million. Adjusted operating margin was 45% compared with 23.9% reported in the year-ago quarter.

The Entertainment segment’s revenues declined 5% year over year to $15.5 million. Our model predicted the segment’s revenues to be $17 million. Adjusted operating margin was 4.5% compared with 1.2% reported in the year-ago quarter.

Story Continues

Operating Highlights of Hasbro

In the fiscal fourth quarter, Hasbro’s cost of sales (as a percentage of net revenues) was 31.3% compared with 32.6% in the year-earlier quarter.

Selling, distribution and administration expenses were $334.2 million compared with $360.6 million reported in the prior-year quarter.

The company reported adjusted EBITDA of $372.2 million compared with $164.8 million a year ago. Our estimate for the metric was $264.5 million.

Hasbro’s Balance Sheet

As of Dec. 28, 2025, cash and cash equivalents were $776.6 million compared with $695 million as of Dec. 29, 2024. At the end of the reported quarter, inventories totaled $259.8 million compared with $274.2 million a year ago.

As of Dec. 28, 2025, long-term debt was $2.8 billion, down from $3.4 billion as of Dec. 29, 2024.

Hasbro 2026 Outlook

For 2025, Hasbro anticipates total revenues to increase 3-5% on a constant currency basis. It continues to expect the adjusted operating margin to be between 24% and 25%.

Adjusted EBITDA is now expected to be in the range of $1.40-$1.45 billion.

How Have Estimates Been Moving Since Then?

In the past month, investors have witnessed a downward trend in fresh estimates.

The consensus estimate has shifted -11.96% due to these changes.

VGM Scores

Currently, Hasbro has a strong Growth Score of A, though it is lagging a lot on the Momentum Score front with a D. Charting a somewhat similar path, the stock has a score of C on the value side, putting it in the middle 20% for this investment strategy.

Overall, the stock has an aggregate VGM Score of B. If you aren't focused on one strategy, this score is the one you should be interested in.

Outlook

Estimates have been broadly trending downward for the stock, and the magnitude of these revisions indicates a downward shift. Interestingly, Hasbro has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). We expect an in-line return from the stock in the next few months.

title: Toy giants face tariffs, spending pressure but at different points in recovery — Wells Fargo
content:
[Monopoly game Go square]
NoDerog/iStock Unreleased via Getty Images

After a lackluster 2025 for the toy industry, Wells Fargo sees the two major players facing similar challenges, namely tariffs and consumer spending trends, but at different stages of recovery.

As the industry leader in core toys, Mattel’s (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) growth has been “nuanced” across its portfolio. However, looking ahead, Wells Fargo analyst Anthony Bonadio believes the deferred KPop tailwinds look material and its doll-heavy portfolio sets the company up for a better 2026.

And while Mattel’s film slate presents a mixed outlook for 2026, Bonadio sees Masters of the Universe as the most likely winner this year with box office estimates at ~$140M (contributing low-single-digit percentage to Mattel’s EBIT).

“An investment year in ’26 is disappointing, but we think the company has cleared the deck and see plenty of upside catalysts remaining in core toys, call options in ancillary businesses, and a constructive early view on 2027,” Bonadio says, assigning an Overweight rating to Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) with a $19 price target that assumes 19% upside from Tuesday’s closing price.

For Hasbro (HAS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAS]), Bonadio sees a “potentially choppy 2026” for the toymaker. The success of its Wizards of the Coast and Monopoly Go! presents tough comparisons for 2026, especially as data shows interest in the Monopoly game waning.

“We are generally constructive on the HAS model,” Bonadio says in this note to clients, but cautions that risks for 2026 have developed, namely the comparison factor in tabletop gaming, easing digital revenue from Monopoly Go!, and deferral of the Exodus video game catalyst into 2027.

Offset by an “interesting opportunity over a longer duration as HAS leverages its IP for gaming,” Bonadio sees a balanced risk/reward for Hasbro and gives the toymaker an Equalweight rating and $98 price target, 3% upside from Tuesday’s closing price.

Shares are mixed with Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) grinding higher, last traded with a 1% gain, while Hasbro (HAS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAS]) is on the defensive, down for a second day.

title: Oracle, Nike upgraded: Wall Street's top analyst calls
content:
The most talked about and market moving research calls around Wall Street are now in one place. Here are today's research calls that investors need to know, as compiled by The Fly.
 Top 5 Upgrades:

JPMorgan upgraded Oracle (ORCL) to Overweight from Neutral with a price target of $210, down from $230, following the fiscal Q3 report. The firm cites the 55% decline in shares since mid-September and Oracle delivering on its growth acceleration for the upgrade. Barclays upgraded Nike (NKE) to Overweight from Equal Weight with a price target of $73, up from $64. The firm cites Nike's operational progress, financial "inflections," and management's "disciplined actions" for the upgrade. Bernstein upgraded J.M. Smucker(SJM) to Outperform from Market Perform with a price target of $145, up from $121. The firm cites the continued deflation in green coffee costs and the arrival of activist Elliott Management for the upgrade. Citi upgraded Intuitive Surgical(ISRG) to Buy from Neutral with an unchanged price target of $590. The firm believes fundamentals in the U.S. medical technology sector remain "healthy" despite the recent volatility. Nomura upgraded Nio (NIO) to Buy from Neutral with a $6.60 price target. Nio is improving from a business and financial perspective over the past two quarters, and the firm believes that Nio is finally entering into a healthy business cycle.

Top 5 Downgrades:

JPMorgan downgraded Day One Biopharmaceuticals(DAWN) to Neutral from Overweight with a $21.50 price target after the company announced an agreement under which Servier will acquire it for $21.50 per share in cash. Raymond James downgraded Optimum Communications(OPTU) to Market Perform from Outperform with no price target. The firm's prior thesis on Optimum was that it would benefit from subscriber growth from lower end customers, but that "has not materialized." Mizuho downgraded VICI Properties(VICI) to Neutral from Outperform with an unchanged price target of $30. The firm cites valuation for the downgrade. Roth Capital downgraded Yext (YEXT) to Neutral from Buy with a price target of $6, down from $9.50, following Q4 results the firm sees highlighting "subpar fundamentals." The firm believes management buyout uncertainty has impacted sales and product execution over past six months and does not expect a tender offer as a positive catalyst in the near-term. SEB Equities downgraded Borr Drilling(BORR) to Hold from Buy with a $5.45 price target. The firm cites valuation for the downgrade, saying the jack-up recovery is reflected in the share price.

Story Continues

Top 5 Initiations:

BofA initiated coverage of Ligand (LGND) with a Buy rating and $244 price target. Citing a portfolio of more than 100 high-value commercial and development assets, increasing needs for flexible, non-dilutive capital in the industry and a lean cost structure, BofA contends that the biopharma royalty firm can continue to deliver on its "aggressive earnings targets." Barclays initiated coverage of Nova (NOVA) with an Overweight rating and $465 price target. As the industry transitions to more metrologically intensive technologies, Nova has "multiple avenues of growth" amid accelerating wafter fab equipment spending, the firm tells investors in a research note. Wells Fargo initiated coverage of Harley-Davidson(HOG) with an Underweight rating and $15 price target. The company has "suffered years of underperformance" and the firm sees "no clear relief in sight" as demand remains poor, dealer health seems challenged, and questions remain around the direction of the business. \Wells Fargo initiated coverage of Hasbro (HAS) with an Equal Weight rating and $98 price target. Wells sees a balanced risk/reward at current share levels. Wells Fargo initiated coverage of Planet Fitness (PLNT) with an Overweight rating and $90 price target. While acknowledging that a new management team and concerns around the next leg of growth "add uncertainty," the firm notes that the stock has retreated, argues that the mid-term outlook screens positive on wellness tailwinds and calls Planet "an impressive growth story."

title: How The Hays (LSE:HAS) Investment Story Is Shifting After Target Cuts And Cautious Outlook
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Hays is back in focus after analysts reset their fair value model slightly lower around £0.64 and one firm cut its price target from £0.61 to £0.44. These moves sit alongside a more cautious stance in recent research, where some analysts question how quickly earnings in Business and Employment Services can recover and whether Hays may sit below earlier expectations for longer. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets fit into the wider narrative and what to watch as sentiment evolves.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Hays shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Hays.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Despite reduced targets, firms such as Morgan Stanley and RBC Capital are still engaged on Hays, which keeps the stock on the radar for investors who focus on where expectations may already reflect a tougher setup. For readers, the cluster of recent reports can be useful if you are looking for fresh views on valuation and earnings power, because it provides updated reference points on how the market is pricing Business and Employment Services risk.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Jefferies moved Hays to Underperform from Hold and lowered its price target to 44 GBp from 61 GBp, highlighting what it sees as cyclical headwinds combined with structural challenges that could keep earnings lower for longer. RBC Capital and Morgan Stanley both cut their price targets in January 2026, which adds to a cautious tone around the pace of any earnings recovery and suggests that some analysts see limited room for positive surprise in the near term.

What's in the News

Hays proposed an interim dividend of 0.15 pence per share for the six months to 31 December 2025, with payment expected on 23 April 2026 to shareholders on the register on 13 March 2026. Chief Executive Officer Dirk Hahn is stepping down for personal reasons on 27 February 2026. Chief Digital and Technology Officer Mark Dearnley will take over as interim CEO while the board searches for a permanent successor. For the second half of 2025, Hays guided to pre exception operating profit of around £20 million, about £5 million lower year on year, alongside a 9% or £45 million net fee decline. The U.K. and Ireland returned to profit, and Australia and New Zealand were up year on year. Earlier in 2026, Hays reported that Dirk Hahn returned to work on 5 January 2026 after a short medical leave. During his absence, Group Chair Michael Findlay acted as Executive Chair and worked with the wider management team on the group’s priorities.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Hays

Fair value revised slightly lower from £0.64 to about £0.64, indicating only a small adjustment in the central estimate. Revenue growth assumption moved from around a 12.9% decline to around a 27.9% decline. Profit margin assumption increased from about 111% to around 170%, which is a very large uplift in the model. Future P/E moved from about 17.8x to about 12.1x. Discount rate shifted from about 8.28% to about 8.70%.

How a greater focus on high demand roles in areas like STEM, technology, and specialist contracting, along with a higher mix of Temp & Contracting and enterprise clients, could support more resilient and recurring fee income. The impact of ongoing investment in recruitment technology, data and AI programs, and cost saving measures that are aimed at improving consultant productivity, margins, and future cash generation. Key risks such as pressure on higher margin Perm recruitment, rising technology and restructuring costs, and potential disruption from digital hiring platforms and AI driven recruitment tools that could challenge the traditional agency model.

title: 3 Mid-Cap Stocks We Approach with Caution
content:
Many investors pay attention to mid-cap stocks because they have established business models and expansive market opportunities. However, their paths to becoming $100 billion corporations are ripe with competition, ranging from giants with vast resources to agile upstarts eager to disrupt the status quo.

This is precisely where StockStory comes in - we do the heavy lifting to identify companies with solid fundamentals so you can invest with confidence. Keeping that in mind, here are three mid-cap stocks to swipe left on and some alternatives you should look into instead.

Tractor Supply (TSCO)

Market Cap: $26.4 billion

Started as a mail-order tractor parts business, Tractor Supply (NASDAQ:TSCO) is a retailer of general goods such as agricultural supplies, hardware, and pet food for the rural consumer.

Why Does TSCO Fall Short?

Scale is a double-edged sword because it limits the company’s growth potential compared to its smaller competitors, as reflected in its below-average annual revenue increases of 3% for the last three years Poor same-store sales performance over the past two years indicates it’s having trouble bringing new shoppers into its brick-and-mortar locations Gross margin of 36.3% is below its competitors, leaving less money for marketing and promotions

Tractor Supply is trading at $50.59 per share, or 23.2x forward P/E. To fully understand why you should be careful with TSCO, check out our full research report (it’s free).

Hasbro (HAS)

Market Cap: $13.29 billion

Credited with the creation of toys such as Mr. Potato Head and the Rubik’s Cube, Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) is a global entertainment company offering a diverse range of toys, games, and multimedia experiences for children and families.

Why Should You Dump HAS?

Sales tumbled by 3% annually over the last five years, showing consumer trends are working against its favor Earnings growth over the last five years fell short of the peer group average as its EPS only increased by 8.1% annually Eroding returns on capital from an already low base indicate that management’s recent investments are destroying value

Hasbro’s stock price of $95.16 implies a valuation ratio of 16.8x forward P/E. Check out our free in-depth research report to learn more about why HAS doesn’t pass our bar.

Bunge Global (BG)

Market Cap: $22.58 billion

With origins dating back to 1818 and operations spanning both hemispheres to balance seasonal harvests, Bunge Global (NYSE:BG) is an agribusiness and food company that processes oilseeds, grains, and other agricultural commodities into vegetable oils, protein meals, flours, and specialty ingredients.

Story Continues

Why Does BG Give Us Pause?

Scale is a double-edged sword because it limits the company’s growth potential compared to its smaller competitors, as reflected in its below-average annual revenue increases of 1.5% for the last three years Performance over the past three years shows its incremental sales were much less profitable, as its earnings per share fell by 19.1% annually Limited cash reserves may force the company to seek unfavorable financing terms that could dilute shareholders

At $117.64 per share, Bunge Global trades at 14.1x forward P/E. If you’re considering BG for your portfolio, see our FREE research report to learn more.

title: Hasbro Announces Pricing of Public Offering of Notes
content:
PAWTUCKET, R.I., March 05, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Hasbro, Inc. (NASDAQ: HAS) ("Hasbro") announced today the pricing of an underwritten registered public offering (the "Offering") of $400,000,000 aggregate principal amount of 4.650% Notes due 2031 (the "Notes"). The Notes will accrue interest beginning March 12, 2026 with semi-annual payments commencing September 12, 2026. Subject to customary closing conditions, the Offering is expected to settle and close on or about March 12, 2026.

The net proceeds from the Offering will be $397 million, after giving effect to the underwriting discount but not estimated offering expenses payable by Hasbro. Hasbro intends to use the net proceeds of the Offering for general corporate purposes, including to redeem or repay outstanding indebtedness.

BofA Securities, Inc., J.P. Morgan Securities LLC, Citigroup Global Markets Inc. and Scotia Capital (USA) Inc. are acting as joint book-running managers for the Offering.

The Offering is being made pursuant to an effective shelf registration statement, including a base prospectus dated May 6, 2024, that has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and is available on the SEC website. A final prospectus supplement describing the terms of the Offering and the accompanying base prospectus will be filed with the SEC and will be available on the SEC website. Copies of these documents may also be obtained by calling BofA Securities, Inc. toll-free at (800) 294-1322, calling J.P. Morgan Securities LLC collect at (212) 834-4533, calling Citigroup Global Markets Inc. toll-free at (800) 831-9146 and calling Scotia Capital (USA) Inc. at (800) 372-3930.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities nor shall there be any sale of securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About Hasbro

Hasbro is a leading games, IP and toy company whose mission is to create joy and community through the magic of play. Hasbro delivers groundbreaking play experiences for fans of all ages around the world, through physical and digital games, video games, toys, licensed consumer products, location-based entertainment, film, TV and more.

Through its franchise-first approach, Hasbro unlocks value from both new and legacy IP, including MAGIC: THE GATHERING, DUNGEONS & DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, HASBRO GAMES, NERF, TRANSFORMERS, PLAY-DOH and PEPPA PIG, as well as premier partner brands. Powered by its portfolio of thousands of iconic marks and a diversified network of partners and subsidiary studios, Hasbro brings fans together wherever they are, from tabletop to screen.

Story Continues

For more than a decade, Hasbro has been consistently recognized for its corporate citizenship, including being named one of the 100 Best Corporate Citizens by 3BL Media.

title: Hasbro Headquarters Shift And FIFA Deal Reframe Investor Story
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Hasbro plans to relocate its headquarters from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, to Boston, with its long held Pawtucket property slated for sale. The company has also announced a new collaboration with FIFA to launch themed tabletop games ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. These moves point to meaningful changes in Hasbro's physical footprint and its approach to global entertainment partnerships.

For investors following Hasbro, NasdaqGS:HAS is trading at $97.63, with the share price up 17.7% year to date and 63.6% over the past year. Over longer periods, returns of 114.2% over three years and 24.4% over five years show that the stock has seen varied performance across time horizons.

The headquarters shift and FIFA partnership could reshape how you think about the mix of real estate, brand partnerships, and product lines behind that share price. As these plans move from announcement to execution, an important question will be how effectively Hasbro converts a new corporate base in Boston and a global football tie up into products and experiences that keep its brands relevant with consumers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Hasbro by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Hasbro.NasdaqGS:HAS Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Mar 2026

📰 Beyond the headline: 3 risks and 2 things going right for Hasbro that every investor should see.

For you as an investor, these announcements sit squarely in how Hasbro wants to position itself as an IP and entertainment company rather than just a traditional toy maker. Moving the headquarters to Boston and selling the long-held Pawtucket site points to a shift in physical footprint that may influence costs, access to talent, and how management allocates capital. At the same time, the FIFA tabletop deal fits with a broader push into branded experiences tied to major events, which could help Hasbro keep pace with peers like Mattel and Spin Master that also lean on licensing and media tie-ins. Execution will matter. Management will need to control relocation expenses, handle any disruption to staff or operations, and make sure the FIFA games reach fans in a crowded World Cup product environment. The expanded revolving credit facility of up to US$1.1b, with a possible US$550.0m incremental increase, gives Hasbro financial flexibility, but it also comes with leverage and interest coverage covenants that investors will want to monitor as these projects roll out.

Story Continues

How This Fits Into The Hasbro Narrative

The FIFA partnership lines up with the existing narrative that branded collaborations and gaming-related products can widen Hasbro’s reach and support higher-margin, repeat-purchase categories. The headquarters move and potential use of the larger credit facility could challenge the narrative if relocation and financing costs pressure profitability, especially while analysts are watching near term earnings and revenue trends. The planned sale of the Pawtucket property and the extended credit line to 2031 introduce real estate and balance sheet angles that are not fully captured in a story focused mainly on digital gaming and franchise IP.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story.  Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Hasbro to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ Relocating the headquarters and exiting Pawtucket could bring one off costs, operational disruption, or talent retention challenges at a time when analysts are already expecting an earnings decline for the upcoming quarter. ⚠️ Greater reliance on event-driven licensed products such as FIFA World Cup titles adds exposure to timing risk, tournament-specific demand, and licensing economics that may not repeat consistently. 🎁 The long term revolving credit facility of US$1.1b, with the option to increase by up to US$550.0m, provides flexibility to fund working capital, product launches, and corporate moves without relying solely on capital markets. 🎁 The FIFA tabletop games give Hasbro another way to lean on its core brands like Monopoly and Connect 4 with a global football audience, which could help keep those franchises relevant to younger and international consumers.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how Hasbro sequences the Boston move, including any disclosed relocation or restructuring charges, and whether management provides clarity on expected savings or benefits. On the product side, monitor how prominently the FIFA games feature in upcoming earnings commentary and guidance, especially around the 2026 World Cup cycle. The revolving credit agreement’s leverage and interest coverage ratios are also worth tracking, as they can limit how aggressively Hasbro uses debt while it reshapes the business. Together, these factors will help you judge whether the physical move and new partnerships are supporting the broader shift toward IP-driven gaming and entertainment or simply adding complexity without clear financial payoff.

title: Hasbro (HAS): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q4 Earnings?
content:
Hasbro’s 21.4% return over the past six months has outpaced the S&P 500 by 15.7%, and its stock price has climbed to $97.61 per share. This was partly thanks to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

Is now the time to buy Hasbro, or should you be careful about including it in your portfolio? See what our analysts have to say in our full research report, it’s free.

Why Do We Think Hasbro Will Underperform?

We’re glad investors have benefited from the price increase, but we don't have much confidence in Hasbro. Here are three reasons there are better opportunities than HAS and a stock we'd rather own.

1. Revenue Spiraling Downwards

Reviewing a company’s long-term sales performance reveals insights into its quality. Any business can have short-term success, but a top-tier one grows for years. Hasbro’s demand was weak over the last five years as its sales fell at a 3% annual rate. This wasn’t a great result and is a sign of poor business quality.Hasbro Quarterly Revenue

2. EPS Barely Growing

We track the long-term change in earnings per share (EPS) because it highlights whether a company’s growth is profitable.

Hasbro’s EPS grew at 8.1% compounded annual growth rate over the last five years. On the bright side, this performance was better than its 3% annualized revenue declines and tells us management adapted its cost structure in response to a challenging demand environment.Hasbro Trailing 12-Month EPS (Non-GAAP)

3. New Investments Fail to Bear Fruit as ROIC Declines

We like to invest in businesses with high returns, but the trend in a company’s ROIC is what often surprises the market and moves the stock price. Over the last few years, Hasbro’s ROIC averaged 3.5 percentage point decreases each year. Paired with its already low returns, these declines suggest its profitable growth opportunities are few and far between.Hasbro Trailing 12-Month Return On Invested Capital

Final Judgment

We cheer for all companies serving everyday consumers, but in the case of Hasbro, we’ll be cheering from the sidelines. With its shares outperforming the market lately, the stock trades at 17.6× forward P/E (or $97.61 per share). This valuation tells us it’s a bit of a market darling with a lot of good news priced in - we think there are better opportunities elsewhere. We’d suggest looking at one of our all-time favorite software stocks.

title: Has Hasbro (HAS) Run Too Far After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

If you are wondering whether Hasbro's share price still offers value after a strong run, or if you might be arriving late to the story, this article walks through what the current valuation is really telling you. Hasbro's stock closed at US$97.63, with returns of 9.3% over the last month, 17.7% year to date and 63.6% over the past year. The last week shows a 2.2% decline that may signal a pause after a bigger move, or a shift in how the market views risk. Recent headlines have focused on Hasbro's position in branded entertainment and consumer products, as the company continues to refresh franchises and licensing partnerships that sit at the core of its business. This context helps frame why the stock has drawn increased attention after a strong multi year return of 114.2% over three years and 24.4% over five years. Simply Wall St currently gives Hasbro a valuation score of 2/6. This suggests there is more to unpack across different models such as discounted cash flow, multiples and peer comparisons. We will also touch on a more holistic way to think about value at the end of this article.

Hasbro scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Hasbro Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash Hasbro might generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to a single value today so you can compare that figure to the current share price.

For Hasbro, the model uses last twelve month Free Cash Flow of about $687.2 million and then applies a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity framework. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extends these with its own extrapolations, including a projected Free Cash Flow of $2,539.4 million in 2035, with each year’s cash flow discounted back to today.

Pulling all of those discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $298.74 per share. Compared with a recent share price of $97.63, the DCF output suggests the stock is 67.3% undervalued on this set of assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Hasbro is undervalued by 67.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 49 more high quality undervalued stocks.HAS Discounted Cash Flow as at Mar 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Hasbro.

Story Continues

Approach 2: Hasbro Price vs Sales

For companies where revenue is a key focus, the price-to-sales (P/S) ratio can be a useful cross check because it compares what you pay for each dollar of sales rather than relying on current earnings, which can be more volatile.

What counts as a normal P/S ratio usually reflects how the market sees a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk often support a higher P/S, while lower growth or higher risk tend to pull that multiple down.

Hasbro currently trades on a P/S of 2.92x. That sits above the Leisure industry average of 0.96x and also above the peer average of 1.15x, which on their own might make the stock look expensive compared with similar companies.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what P/S might be reasonable for Hasbro given factors such as its growth outlook, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk profile. Because it blends these elements together, it can be more tailored than a simple comparison with industry or peer averages.

Hasbro’s Fair Ratio is 2.30x, which is below the current P/S of 2.92x. This indicates the shares may be trading above the level implied by this metric.

Result: OVERVALUEDNasdaqGS:HAS P/S Ratio as at Mar 2026

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 18 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Hasbro Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page where you connect your view of Hasbro’s story to a set of forecasts and a fair value, then compare that fair value to today’s price. The Narrative automatically refreshes when new news or earnings arrive.

In practice, one Hasbro Narrative on the platform currently anchors on concerns about brand health, management decisions and debt levels and arrives at a fair value of about US$1.90 per share. Another focuses on digital gaming, licensing income and margin execution and lands at about US$113.38 per share. This shows how two investors looking at the same company and data can reach very different conclusions that the tool makes explicit and easy to compare.

For Hasbro, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Hasbro Narratives:

Both are looking at the same company and price, yet they reach very different conclusions about what the stock is worth and how the story could play out. Your own view may sit somewhere between these two or lean firmly to one side.

🐂 Hasbro Bull Case

Fair value: US$113.38 per share

Implied discount to this fair value at US$97.63: about 13.9% undervalued

Revenue growth used in this narrative: 4.63% a year

Backs higher earnings potential from digital gaming, licensing and media tie ins, with Wizards of the Coast and brands like Magic: The Gathering and Dungeons & Dragons playing a central role. Assumes efficiency gains, cost rationalisation and a focus on higher margin franchise IP support stronger profit margins over time. Uses analyst forecasts for revenue, earnings and a future P/E in the low 20s, then discounts those cash flows using a 7.6% rate to reach the fair value.

🐻 Hasbro Bear Case

Fair value: US$1.90 per share

Implied premium to this fair value at US$97.63: very large overvaluation

Revenue trend used in this narrative: 93% revenue decline assumption

Argues that many of Hasbro's core toy and game brands are weakened on the ground at retailers, with limited shelf presence and quality concerns across several lines. Points to management, capital allocation and brand stewardship as key issues, including past decisions around Entertainment One, layoffs and continued dividend payments alongside higher debt. Frames the business as structurally challenged, with heavy liabilities and brand mismanagement, and therefore assigns a very low fair value despite the portfolio of IP.

These two Narratives show the range of outcomes that different investors see when they connect the same data to very different assumptions and risk views. If you want to test where your own view fits on that spectrum and how it lines up with detailed forecasts and valuation work, you can read both Narratives in full and see how the numbers change as the story evolves over time: Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? title: Hasbro (HAS) Sees a More Significant Dip Than Broader Market: Some Facts to Know
content:
In the latest close session, Hasbro (HAS) was down 1.43% at $97.63. This move lagged the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.94%. At the same time, the Dow lost 0.83%, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq lost 1.02%.

The stock of toy maker has risen by 5.55% in the past month, leading the Consumer Discretionary sector's gain of 1.25% and the S&P 500's loss of 1.3%.

Analysts and investors alike will be keeping a close eye on the performance of Hasbro in its upcoming earnings disclosure. In that report, analysts expect Hasbro to post earnings of $0.97 per share. This would mark a year-over-year decline of 6.73%. At the same time, our most recent consensus estimate is projecting a revenue of $913.69 million, reflecting a 3% rise from the equivalent quarter last year.

For the annual period, the Zacks Consensus Estimates anticipate earnings of $5.67 per share and a revenue of $4.94 billion, signifying shifts of +2.35% and +5.16%, respectively, from the last year.

Any recent changes to analyst estimates for Hasbro should also be noted by investors. Recent revisions tend to reflect the latest near-term business trends. Hence, positive alterations in estimates signify analyst optimism regarding the business and profitability.

Our research suggests that these changes in estimates have a direct relationship with upcoming stock price performance. We developed the Zacks Rank to capitalize on this phenomenon. Our system takes these estimate changes into account and delivers a clear, actionable rating model.

The Zacks Rank system, ranging from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), possesses a remarkable history of outdoing, externally audited, with #1 stocks returning an average annual gain of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate has moved 4.14% higher. Hasbro is holding a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) right now.

Looking at its valuation, Hasbro is holding a Forward P/E ratio of 17.49. This expresses a premium compared to the average Forward P/E of 12.51 of its industry.

It's also important to note that HAS currently trades at a PEG ratio of 1.71. Comparable to the widely accepted P/E ratio, the PEG ratio also accounts for the company's projected earnings growth. The Toys - Games - Hobbies was holding an average PEG ratio of 2 at yesterday's closing price.

The Toys - Games - Hobbies industry is part of the Consumer Discretionary sector. With its current Zacks Industry Rank of 232, this industry ranks in the bottom 6% of all industries, numbering over 250.

The Zacks Industry Rank gauges the strength of our individual industry groups by measuring the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within the groups. Our research shows that the top 50% rated industries outperform the bottom half by a factor of 2 to 1.

title: Norwegian Cruise Q4 Earnings Meet Estimates, Revenues Lag, Stock Down
content:
Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, with earnings meeting the Zacks Consensus Estimate, and revenues missing the same. The top and bottom lines increased on a year-over-year basis.

Following the results, the company’s shares declined 7.3% in the pre-market trading session. Negative investor sentiments were witnessed on account of macro concerns tied to rising oil prices amid Middle East tensions. Also, soft bookings, stemming from execution missteps in aligning its commercial strategy with deployment, added to the downside.

NCLH’s Q4 Earnings & Revenues

Norwegian Cruise reported an adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of 28 cents, in line with the Zacks Consensus Estimate. In the prior-year quarter, the company reported an adjusted EPS of 19 cents.

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. Price, Consensus and EPS SurpriseNorwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. price-consensus-eps-surprise-chart | Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. Quote

Quarterly revenues of $2.24 billion missed the consensus mark of $2.35 billion. The metric increased 6.4% year over year.

Passenger ticket revenues were $1.51 billion compared with $1.4 billion reported in the prior-year quarter. Our model anticipated passenger ticket revenues to be $1.66 billion.

Onboard and other revenues increased to $734.4 million from $700.6 million reported in the prior-year quarter. We expected onboard and other revenues to be $674.8 million.

NCLH’s Expenses & Operating Results

Total cruise operating expenses in the fourth quarter increased 1.2% year over year to $1.32 billion. Our model anticipated total cruise operating expenses to be $1.41 billion.

During the fourth quarter, gross cruise costs per Capacity Day were $272 compared with $286 reported in the prior-year period. Adjusted net cruise costs (excluding fuel) per Capacity Day amounted to about $159, up 0.9% year over year on a reported basis.

Net interest expenses were $170 million, down from $175.4 million reported in the year-ago quarter.

NCLH’s Balance Sheet

As of Dec. 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $209.9 million, down from $190.8 million at the end of 2024. Long-term debt was $13.7 billion compared with $11.8 billion as of 2024-end.

2025 Highlights

Total revenues in 2025 came in at $9.83 billion compared with $9.48 billion reported in 2024.

Adjusted EBITDA in 2025 came in at $2.73 billion compared with $2.45 million reported in 2024.

In 2025, adjusted earnings per share came in at $2.11 compared with $1.77 reported in the previous year.

Story Continues

Booking Update of NCLH

The company heads into 2026 facing a somewhat pressured environment, with bookings trending slightly below its optimal range following execution gaps in aligning commercial strategy with deployment plans. Fourth-quarter results reflect the impact of a meaningful capacity increase in the Caribbean, even as longer-term demand fundamentals remain supportive. Deployment adjustments are beginning to yield results, driving improved load factors. Fourth-quarter occupancy reached 101.8%, up 100 basis points year over year.

Demand has been especially resilient across the company’s luxury portfolio, supported by longer booking curves. Notably, Oceania Cruises achieved record bookings upon opening sales for its newest ship, Oceania Sonata, scheduled to debut in August 2027. Meanwhile, Regent Seven Seas Cruises posted the strongest booking month in its history in January, underscoring continued momentum in the high-end segment.

Q1 & 2026 Guidance by NCLH

For first-quarter 2026, NCLH anticipates occupancy to be approximately 104.2% and Capacity Days to be about 6.39 million. For the quarter, interest expenses are expected to be approximately $170 million, while depreciation and amortization are anticipated to be about $260 million. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be about $515 million. Adjusted EPS is predicted to be 16 cents.

For 2026, the company anticipates occupancy to be approximately 105.7% and Capacity Days to be about 26.25 million. During the year, interest expenses are expected to be approximately $708 million. Depreciation and amortization are anticipated at nearly $1.09 billion. Adjusted EBITDA during the year is expected to be nearly $2.95 billion. For 2026, NCLH expects adjusted EPS of $2.38.

NCLH’s Zacks Rank & Recent Consumer Discretionary Releases

Norwegian Cruise currently has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. HLT reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, wherein earnings and revenues surpassed the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top and bottom lines increased on a year-over-year basis.

Hilton’s fourth-quarter 2025 results were supported by steady demand trends, year-over-year RevPAR growth and continued expansion of its global footprint. The company added new hotels during the quarter and delivered strong net unit growth while maintaining a robust development pipeline that provides solid long-term visibility. Continued expansion of its luxury and lifestyle portfolio, along with new brand launches such as the Apartment Collection by Hilton, also contributed positively to overall performance.

Marriott International, Inc. MAR reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, with adjusted earnings missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate and revenues beating the same. The top and bottom lines increased on a year-over-year basis.

Marriott delivered steady performance, supported by resilient rooms’ growth, pricing strength and continued development momentum. Global RevPAR posted modest growth, led by stronger international markets, while luxury properties continued to outperform on the back of healthy demand and favorable rates. Despite relatively stable performance in the United States & Canada, Marriott maintained a premium RevPAR compared with peers, reflecting the strength of its diversified brand portfolio and asset-light business model.

Hasbro, Inc. HAS reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results, with earnings and revenues beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top and bottom lines increased year over year. 

Hasbro’s management highlighted a strong 2025, with it returning to growth on the back of disciplined execution and the “Playing to Win” strategy. Leadership emphasized broader fan engagement, new partnerships and meaningful progress toward becoming a more digital and IP-focused business, setting a confident tone for Hasbro for 2026.

title: Alphabet Upgraded, United Airlines Downgraded: Updated Rankings on Top Blue-Chip Stocks

title: Rush Street Interactive CEO Sells Nearly 250k Shares For Over $40M
content:
Richard Todd Schwartz, Chief Executive Officer of Rush Street Interactive(NYSE:RSI), reported the sale of 247,114 shares of Common Stock for a total consideration of ~$4.16 million on Feb. 17, 2026, according to a SEC Form 4 filing.

Transaction summary

Metric Value Shares sold (direct) 247,114 Transaction value $4.16 million Post-transaction shares (direct) 701,934 Post-transaction value (direct ownership) $11.89 million

Transaction value based on SEC Form 4 reported price ($16.82); post-transaction value based on Feb. 17, 2026 market close ($16.94).

Key questions

How does the size of this sale compare to Schwartz’s recent transaction history?
This transaction ties for Schwartz’s largest sale, in which he sold the exact number of shares on Feb. 4. What percentage of Schwartz’s remaining direct holdings does this sale represent, and how has capacity influenced recent trade sizes?
This transaction accounted for 26.0% of Schwartz’s direct holdings immediately prior to the sale.

Company overview

Metric Value Revenue (TTM) $1.13 billion Net income (TTM) $33.31 million Employees 912 1-year price change (Feb. 28, 2026) 48.50%

Company snapshot

Rush Street Interactive operates as an online casino and sports betting company in the United States and Latin America. The company markets its online casino and sports betting services under the BetRivers.com, PlaySugarHouse.com, and RushBet.co brands.

What this transaction means for investors

In Q3 fiscal year 2025, Rush Street had its tenth consecutive quarter of improved revenue, beating analysts’ revenue expectations by 4.3%. Many expected the company would deliver another successful earnings report for Q4, and on Feb. 17, 2026, it indeed did.

Quarterly revenue grew yet again, and annual revenue reached $1 billion for the first time. Its annual earnings per share (EPS) grew approximately 973% year over year (YoY). The growth is substantial compared to previous years, when the company consistently operated at a net income and EPS deficit.

Other companies within the casino and betting industry have also reported positive results in their recent earnings reports. This includes DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG), and Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS), which is historically known as a toy manufacturer, but in July 2025, began venturing into the online casino industry with multiple licensing deals.

RSI stock rose approximately 40% in 2025, and after a strong close to its fiscal year, it looks poised for long-term growth, especially as the online betting industry currently booms.

Should you buy stock in Rush Street Interactive right now?

title: FedEx, EssilorLuxottica sued by customers for tariff refunds
content:
[US Tariffs Legal Challenges]

Shipping giant FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) and French eyewear firm EssilorLuxottica (ESLOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ESLOY]) are facing lawsuits from customers seeking a portion of refunds from the Trump administration’s emergency tariffs that the U.S. Supreme Court invalidated last week.

The proposed class action lawsuits filed against FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) in Florida and EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ESLOF]) in New York this week aim to capitalize on reimbursements that the businesses are eligible to receive as part of a future refund process.

"If refunds are issued to FedEx, we will issue refunds to the shippers and consumers who originally bore those charges,” Reuters reported, quoting FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]).

The lawsuit filed in the federal court in Miami, Florida, by plaintiff Matthew Reiser against FDX states that the company’s pledge to issue refunds “creates no legally enforceable obligation and is expressly contingent on future government and court guidance that may never materialize.”

In a complaint filed against EssilorLuxottica (ESLOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ESLOY]), Nathan Ward of New York argued that the Ray-Ban sunglasses he purchased from ray-ban.com [https://ray-ban.com] in August 2025 were priced higher than in the past due to a tariff surcharge.

“Despite seeking an order entitling it to a refund of the duties collected as a result of the subject tariffs, EssilorLuxottica continues to collect and has not refunded the tariff surcharges it collected from consumers,” the lawsuit says.

The claims come at a time when at least 2,000 firms, including FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]), are suing the government to recover duties that they paid under President Donald Trump’s tariffs, which the Supreme Court, in a 6-3 ruling, found illegal on Feb. 20. [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4554697-us-supreme-court-strikes-down-trumps-global-tariffs#hasComeFromMpArticle=false]

This week, toymaker Hasbro (HAS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAS]), French beauty group L'Oréal (LRLCF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LRLCF]) (LRLCY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LRLCY]), and Canadian eye care company Bausch + Lomb (BLCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLCO]) became the latest to file lawsuits in the U.S. Court of International Trade seeking tariff-related refunds.

title: Toymaker Hasbro joins wave of companies suing U.S. government over IEEPA tariff payments
content:
By Arriana McLymore

NEW YORK, NY, Feb 27 - Toymaker Hasbro on Friday joined ‌hundreds of companies suing for refunds ‌of tariffs paid under President Donald Trump’s emergency ​trade measures, which were deemed illegal by the U.S. Supreme Court last week, according to court documents.

The Play-Doh-maker, which did not ‌disclose how much ⁠it has paid in IEEPA tariffs, is requesting full refunds along ⁠with interest. The lawsuit joins more than 2,000 similar cases filed in the U.S. ​Court of ​International Trade since ​April.

French beauty group ‌L'Oreal , British vacuum manufacturer Dyson and contact lens maker Bausch + Lomb on Monday filed lawsuits against U.S. Customs and Border Protection over IEEPA refunds. Beanie Babies maker Ty Inc. ‌also filed a similar ​lawsuit this week.

Hasbro is being ​represented by law ​firm Sandler, Travis & Rosenberg, which ‌is also representing Swiss ​sportswear company ​On and personal care product maker Conair in their respective IEEPA lawsuits.

Hasbro did not ​immediately respond ‌to a request for comment.

(Reporting by ​Arriana McLymore in New York City; ​Editing by Chizu Nomiyama)

title: Why The Hays (LSE:HAS) Narrative Is Shifting After Price Target Cuts And New Guidance
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Hays is back in focus after bearish analysts trimmed price targets by £0.06 and £0.10, with one high profile move taking the target to £0.44. Those cuts sit alongside an Underperform rating and cautious research that questions how quickly cyclical and structural pressures might ease. Read on to see how you can track these shifting views and what to watch as the narrative around Hays continues to evolve.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Hays shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Hays.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Even with cuts, Morgan Stanley and RBC Capital are still assigning explicit price targets, which signals that they see a basis for valuing Hays on ongoing earnings power rather than treating it as a broken story. The sector discussion in the Jefferies research suggests investors are actively comparing Hays with other European employment services peers, which can help support interest in the shares when sentiment improves toward the group.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Jefferies moved Hays to Underperform from Hold and cut its price target to £0.44 from £0.61, highlighting concerns that cyclical headwinds and structural challenges could keep earnings lower for longer. Morgan Stanley and RBC Capital both reduced their price targets, which points to a more cautious stance on execution and growth prospects as analysts reassess what they are willing to pay for Hays shares.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!LSE:HAS 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for Hays. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Hays issued earnings guidance for the second half of 2025, with pre-exceptional operating profit expected to be around £20 million, about £5 million lower year on year, alongside a 9% net fee decline of about £45 million. Within that guidance, Hays reported that the U.K. and Ireland are back in profit and that Australia and New Zealand are up year on year, despite the 9% net fee decline. Chief Executive Officer Dirk Hahn returned to work on 5 January 2026 after a short medical leave that began in November. During this period, Group Chair Michael Findlay served as Executive Chair to maintain continuity on Group priorities.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Hays

Fair Value: The central estimate remains at £0.64 with no change in the model output. Revenue Growth: The assumed revenue change is effectively the same at about a 13% decline. Net Profit Margin: The implied profit margin remains close to 111% with no material adjustment. P/E Ratio: The assumed future P/E moves slightly from 17.9x to about 17.8x. Discount Rate: The discount rate edges up from 8.21% to about 8.28% in the updated model.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company’s story to a financial forecast and fair value, so you can see how business changes connect to the numbers that analysts use. They refresh as new data and research come through, giving you a living view of the investment case.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Hays to stay up to date on:

How Hays is tilting towards high demand areas such as STEM, technology and specialist contracting roles, and increasing exposure to Temp & Contracting and enterprise clients to support more recurring fee income. The role of ongoing investment in digital tools, data and AI programs, along with cost saving measures and pension de-risking, in reshaping efficiency and cash generation potential. Key risks around structurally weaker Perm recruitment volumes, higher technology and restructuring costs, and competition from digital and AI driven hiring models that could pressure margins and long term fee income.

title: United States Action Figures Market Report 2025-2033 Featuring LEGO, Bandai Namco, Diamond Select Toys, Good Smile, Playmates Toys, Spin Master, Hasbro, Mattel, McFarlane Toys, and JAKKS Pacific
content:
Company Logo

The United States action figures market is set to grow significantly from US$ 3.16 billion in 2025 to US$ 5.25 billion by 2033, at a CAGR of 6.53%. This growth is fueled by fandom culture, collectibles demand, and strong media influence including movies, comics, and video games. The shift towards e-commerce enhances product accessibility and consumer engagement. Despite challenges like digital entertainment competition and raw material cost volatility, the market thrives on innovation, storytelling, and franchise tie-ins. Key players like Mattel and Hasbro continue to innovate, focusing on digital and physical integrations to captivate both children and adult collectors.

U.S. Action Figures MarketU.S. Action Figures Market·GlobeNewswire Inc.

Dublin, Feb. 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The "United States Action Figures Market Report by Type, Distribution Channel, End User, States and Company Analysis, 2025-2033" has been added to  ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

The United States Action Figures Market is expected to reach US$ 5.25 billion by 2033 from US$ 3.16 billion in 2025, with a CAGR of 6.53% from 2025 to 2033

The United States action figures market is anticipated to witness steady growth, driven by rising fandom culture, collectibles demand, movie franchises, and expanding e-commerce channels supporting diverse consumer preferences.

The United States action figures industry has evolved significantly, becoming a prominent segment within the broader toy and collectibles market. Increasing influence from popular media, including movies, comic books, and video games, has driven strong consumer engagement. The market caters to both children and adult collectors, with adult fans representing a rapidly growing segment driven by nostalgia and investment potential. Leading brands continuously innovate with premium-quality, detailed, and limited-edition figures to attract enthusiasts and capitalize on franchise popularity. Digital transformation, coupled with the rise of e-commerce and online communities, has also played a crucial role in shaping buying behavior and market expansion.

The U.S. market benefits from strong entertainment and licensing ecosystems, as action figures tied to blockbuster franchises dominate retail sales. Manufacturers are focusing on developing multi-platform engagement strategies, including digital integrations and augmented reality features, to enhance consumer experience. Collaborations with entertainment studios and content creators have led to cross-promotional opportunities, ensuring sustained brand visibility. Furthermore, customization trends and direct-to-consumer models are reshaping how companies interact with buyers, creating new growth pathways for niche and collector-oriented offerings.

Story Continues

Despite its promising outlook, the market faces challenges related to fluctuating raw material costs and the growing preference for digital entertainment among younger audiences. Nonetheless, innovation, storytelling integration, and enhanced retail accessibility continue to define the U.S. action figures landscape. As technology and fandom culture converge, the market is expected to maintain strong momentum, supported by continuous product diversification and loyal collector communities across the country.

Key Factors Driving the United States Action Figures Market Growth

Influence of Entertainment Franchises and Media

The expanding influence of blockbuster movies, television series, comic book universes, and video games has been a major growth catalyst for the U.S. action figures market. Iconic characters from superhero, fantasy, and sci-fi franchises fuel consistent consumer demand. The integration of storytelling with product design enhances emotional connection and repeat purchases.

Licensing collaborations between toy manufacturers and entertainment studios amplify visibility through coordinated marketing campaigns. Additionally, the release of new film installments and streaming series revitalizes interest in existing product lines. This cross-industry synergy ensures a steady pipeline of new figures and limited editions, appealing to both children and adult collectors. As entertainment consumption continues to rise across platforms, the cultural impact of these franchises remains a pivotal force in sustaining market growth.

Growing Collectible Culture Among Adults

Adult collectors have become a major driving force in the U.S. action figures market, shifting the industry from mere playthings to valuable collectibles. Nostalgia, emotional connection, and investment potential are central motivators. Manufacturers now target this demographic by offering exclusive, high-detail, and limited-edition products that cater to mature audiences. Online marketplaces and collector forums have amplified secondary market activity, with rare figures commanding premium prices.

The growing appeal of pop culture conventions and fan events also contributes to strong brand engagement. Additionally, partnerships between toy companies and entertainment franchises create ongoing opportunities for collector-oriented releases. This expanding adult fanbase reinforces long-term stability in demand, transforming action figures into lifestyle and cultural commodities beyond the traditional toy sector.

Expansion of E-Commerce and Digital Marketing Channels

E-commerce has revolutionized the U.S. action figures market, providing consumers with broader product accessibility and brand interaction. Online platforms offer extensive catalogues, including limited releases and international exclusives, attracting both casual buyers and serious collectors. Digital marketing, social media engagement, and influencer collaborations have enhanced visibility and brand loyalty.

Retailers benefit from analytics-driven marketing strategies that personalize consumer experiences and predict demand trends. Moreover, direct-to-consumer (D2C) platforms enable brands to control distribution, pricing, and customer engagement more effectively. The integration of augmented reality (AR) previews and interactive shopping experiences has further boosted digital sales. This digital transformation continues to drive market penetration, especially among tech-savvy and younger demographics who prefer online purchasing convenience.

Challenges in the United States Action Figures Market

Rising Competition from Digital Entertainment

The growing popularity of digital gaming, streaming services, and virtual collectibles presents a key challenge to the U.S. action figures market. Younger consumers increasingly gravitate toward interactive and digital experiences, reducing engagement with physical toys. Video games and virtual collectibles offer instant gratification and social interaction, diverting attention from traditional play patterns.

To remain relevant, manufacturers must innovate through hybrid experiences that merge physical toys with digital integration, such as augmented reality features or app-based extensions. Additionally, maintaining consumer interest amid rapidly evolving entertainment trends requires continuous creativity and brand collaborations. While digital media competition remains intense, brands adapting to this evolving landscape through storytelling and technology-driven engagement have the potential to sustain long-term relevance.

Volatility in Raw Material and Production Costs

Fluctuations in raw material prices, particularly plastics and packaging materials, directly affect manufacturing costs in the action figures market. Global supply chain disruptions, inflationary pressures, and transportation challenges further complicate production and distribution. As profit margins tighten, companies must balance between maintaining product quality and managing affordability.

Additionally, sustainability concerns regarding plastic waste have prompted calls for eco-friendly alternatives, increasing research and development expenses. The shift toward sustainable manufacturing adds complexity to cost structures, requiring strategic adaptation and supplier diversification. Despite these challenges, many manufacturers are investing in recyclable materials, 3D printing technologies, and localized production to mitigate risk. However, persistent cost volatility remains a key restraint affecting pricing stability and profitability across the U.S. market.

title: Inspired, LKQ, Hilton, Royal Caribbean, and Hasbro Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
content:
What Happened?

A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after the Trump administration's announcement of new global tariffs, reignited trade policy uncertainty.

The move came swiftly after the Supreme Court ruled the previous week that the president could not use the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) for such duties, a decision that had initially sent markets higher. However, the administration invoked a different authority, the Trade Act of 1974, to impose a 15% global tariff for up to 150 days. The rapid reimposition of trade barriers creates significant uncertainty for companies across multiple sectors that depend on international supply chains and global trade. Investors are now weighing the potential impact of these new duties on corporate earnings and broader economic activity.

The stock market overreacts to news, and big price drops can present good opportunities to buy high-quality stocks.

Among others, the following stocks were impacted:

Consumer Discretionary - Gaming Solutions company Inspired (NASDAQ:INSE) fell 3.7%. Is now the time to buy Inspired? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Consumer Discretionary - Specialized Consumer Services company LKQ (NASDAQ:LKQ) fell 3.2%. Is now the time to buy LKQ? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Consumer Discretionary - Travel and Vacation Providers company Hilton (NYSE:HLT) fell 2.3%. Is now the time to buy Hilton? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Consumer Discretionary - Travel and Vacation Providers company Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL) fell 2.9%. Is now the time to buy Royal Caribbean? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Consumer Discretionary - Toys and Electronics company Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) fell 4.4%. Is now the time to buy Hasbro? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free.

Zooming In On Hasbro (HAS)

Hasbro’s shares are not very volatile and have only had 7 moves greater than 5% over the last year. In that context, today’s move indicates the market considers this news meaningful, although it might not be something that would fundamentally change its perception of the business.

The biggest move we wrote about over the last year was 10 months ago when the stock gained 16.1% on the news that the company reported strong first quarter 2025 results which significantly exceeded analysts' expectations across revenue, EPS, and EBITDA. The real spark came from Magic: The Gathering and other digital games, which pushed the gaming unit up 46%, a big jump that drove the whole business. Zooming out, we think this quarter featured some important positives.

Story Continues

Hasbro is up 17% since the beginning of the year, and at $97.07 per share, it is trading close to its 52-week high of $105.94 from February 2026. Investors who bought $1,000 worth of Hasbro’s shares 5 years ago would now be looking at an investment worth $1,062.

While Wall Street chases Nvidia at all-time highs, an under-the-radar semiconductor supplier is dominating a critical AI component these giants can’t build without. Click here to access our full research report, it’s free.

title: US stocks end higher after Supreme Court rules against Trump tariffs
content:
STORY: Wall Street's main indexes ended higher on Friday, with the Dow adding nearly half a percent, the S&P 500 gaining about seven-tenths of a percent and the Nasdaq climbing nine-tenths of a percent.The U.S. Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's global tariffs, in a rebuke of one of his key economic policies.Trump called the ruling a "disgrace" and said he would impose a 10% global tariff for 150 days under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974.Anna Rathbun is founder and CEO of Grenadilla Advisory.“The markets are mostly in the green and have been throughout the day. And honestly, that is the market feeling a bit of a burden coming off of its shoulders. But it has been moving sideways during the day, not going straight up. And the reason for that, I think, is because it's still uncertain. Just because a ruling came in striking down Mr. Trump's tariffs, that doesn't mean that we're not going to have additional tariffs - and we just heard today that we will. And 150 days later, things might change again. So it makes the future uncertain.”Some of Wall Street's most valuable and widely held companies rose, including a more than 3.5% rise in Google-parent Alphabet. Shares of Amazon and Apple also rallied.Shares of several retailers hit by tariffs also gained, including toymaker Hasbro, online furniture retailer Wayfair, Pottery Barn-owner Williams-Sonoma and luxury furniture seller RH.Among other movers, shares of Akamai Technologies slumped 14% after the cloud company forecast first-quarter adjusted profit below Wall Street estimates.Meanwhile, data released early in the day showed U.S. economic growth slowed more than expected in the fourth quarter, while a separate reading indicated inflation picked up in December.Traders see just over a 50% chance the Fed will cut interest rates by its June policy meeting, according to CME's FedWatch Tool.

title: Live Nation's Q4 Earnings & Revenues Beat Estimates, Stock Up
content:
Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. LYV reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, with earnings and revenues beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top line increased year over year, while the bottom line declined from the prior-year quarter’s figure.

Live Nation reported steady performance, supported by strong global touring demand, rising fan engagement and expanding venue infrastructure. Management stated that strengthening connections between artists and global audiences, alongside expansion into international markets, underpinned another year of strong performance. It emphasized that targeted investments in premium venues and production capabilities are enhancing artist experiences while supporting long-term earnings visibility.

Following the results, the company's shares gained 3.5% in the after-hours trading session yesterday.

LYV’s Q4 Earnings & Revenues

The company reported an adjusted loss per share of $1.06, narrower than the Zacks Consensus Estimate of a loss of $1.08. In the year-ago quarter, it reported an adjusted earnings per share of 56 cents.

Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. Price, Consensus and EPS SurpriseLive Nation Entertainment, Inc. Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise

Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. price-consensus-eps-surprise-chart | Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. Quote

Revenues of $6.31 billion beat the consensus mark of $6.07 billion. The top line increased 11% year over year on a reported basis.

Live Nation’s Q4 Segmental Discussion

Concerts: The segment’s fourth-quarter revenues totaled $5.15 billion, up 12% year over year. Adjusted operating loss came in at $192.3 million, compared with a loss of $213.2 million reported in the prior-year quarter.

Ticketing: Segmental revenues amounted to $846.2 million, up 1% from the prior-year quarter. Adjusted operating income was $305.3 million, down 2% from $311.2 million reported in the prior-year quarter.

Sponsorship & Advertising: Revenues from this segment totaled $329.9 million, up 17% from the year-ago quarter’s figure. Adjusted operating income of $168.6 million was up 24% year over year.

Other Financial Information of LYV

Live Nation's cash and cash equivalents, as of Dec. 31, 2025, totaled $7.09 billion, up from $6.1 billion as of Dec. 31, 2024. At the end of the fourth quarter, goodwill was $2.89 billion compared with $2.62 billion at the end of 2024. Net long-term debt as of Dec. 31, 2025, was $7.61 billion, compared with $6.18 billion as of Dec. 31, 2024.

For the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2025, net cash provided by operating activities was $1.39 billion compared with $1.73 billion reported in the year-ago period. As of the said time frame, adjusted free cash flow was $1.29 billion, slightly up from $1.15 billion in the year-ago period.

Story Continues

LYV’s 2025 Financial Highlights

In 2025, revenues increased 9% year over year to $25.2 billion, while operating income jumped 52% year over year to $1.3 billion. Adjusted operating income (AOI) rose 10% year over year to $2.4 billion. Concerts remained the primary growth driver, with a record AOI of $687 million and margins reaching 3.3%. Global attendance climbed 5% year over year to 159 million fans, supported by stronger stadium activity and international markets. Sponsorship AOI advanced 11% year over year to $845 million, and ticketing gross transaction value rose 9% year over year to $26 billion.

2026 Outlook by LYV

Looking ahead, Venue Nation is projected to host more than 70 million fans in 2026, representing high-single- to low-double-digit growth year over year. Large venues scheduled to open through construction and acquisitions are expected to add 5 to 7 million incremental fans annually once fully ramped, with more than half located outside the United States. Planned additions include two U.S. amphitheaters, a new stadium in Mexico, and arena acquisitions across Europe and Latin America, including La Défense.

Pre-opening costs for venues currently under development are projected at approximately $50 million in 2026. Properties opening that year are expected to reach run-rate annual AOI levels by 2029, also targeting returns above 20%.

Management expects sponsorship AOI growth in 2026 to be driven largely by continued expansion of the global venue portfolio and rising demand for festival partnerships, supported by brands seeking broader access to live audiences.

Capital expenditures for 2026 are projected to be between $1.1 billion and $1.2 billion, with roughly $800 million to $850 million allocated to venue development and upgrades. Funding support from joint-venture partners, sponsorship agreements and other sources is expected to reduce venue investment cash requirements by about $250 million.

On the cost side, depreciation and amortization expense is forecast to increase about 10%, while net interest expense is expected to total roughly $280 million. Tax expense is projected at 15-20% of AOI, with cash taxes estimated at about 80% of that level due to timing differences and tax benefits across markets. Share count is not expected to change materially from 2025 levels.

LYV’s Zacks Rank & Recent Consumer Discretionary Releases

Live Nation currently carries a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. HLT reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, wherein earnings and revenues surpassed the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top and bottom lines increased on a year-over-year basis.

Hilton’s fourth-quarter 2025 results were supported by steady demand trends, year-over-year RevPAR growth and continued expansion of its global footprint. The company added new hotels during the quarter and delivered strong net unit growth while maintaining a robust development pipeline that provides solid long-term visibility. Continued expansion of its luxury and lifestyle portfolio, along with new brand launches such as the Apartment Collection by Hilton, also contributed positively to overall performance.

Marriott International, Inc. MAR reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, with adjusted earnings missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate and revenues beating the same. The top and bottom lines increased on a year-over-year basis.

Marriott delivered steady performance, supported by resilient rooms’ growth, pricing strength and continued development momentum. Global RevPAR posted modest growth, led by stronger international markets, while luxury properties continued to outperform on the back of healthy demand and favorable rates. Despite relatively stable performance in the United States & Canada, Marriott maintained a premium RevPAR compared with peers, reflecting the strength of its diversified brand portfolio and asset-light business model.

Hasbro, Inc. HAS reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results, with earnings and revenues beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The top and bottom lines increased year over year. 

Hasbro’s management highlighted a strong 2025, with it returning to growth on the back of disciplined execution and the “Playing to Win” strategy. Leadership emphasized broader fan engagement, new partnerships and meaningful progress toward becoming a more digital and IP-focused business, setting a confident tone for Hasbro for 2026.
